segments.text: This is the blessing that Moses the man of God pronounced upon the Israelites before his death.
He said:

The LORD came from Sinai and dawned upon us from Seir; He shone forth from Mount Paran and came with myriads of holy ones, 
with flaming fire at His right hand.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Surely You love the people; all the holy ones are in Your hand, and they sit down at Your feet;  each receives Your words—
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: the law that Moses gave us, the possession of the assembly of Jacob.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
So the LORD became King in Jeshurun when the leaders of the people gathered, 
when the tribes of Israel came together.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Let Reuben live and not die, nor his men be few.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: And concerning Judah he said:
O LORD, hear the cry of Judah and bring him to his people. With his own hands he defends his cause, but may You be a help against his foes.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Concerning Levi he said:
Give Your Thummim to Levi and Your Urim to Your godly one,  whom You tested at Massah and contested at the waters of Meribah.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: He said of his father and mother,‘I do not consider them.’He disregarded his brothers and did not know his own sons, for he kept Your word and maintained Your covenant.
segments.speaker: Levi

segments.text: He will teach Your ordinances to Jacob and Your law to Israel; he will set incense before You and whole burnt offerings on Your altar.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Bless his substance, O LORD, and accept the work of his hands. Smash the loins of those who rise against him, and of his foes so they can rise no more.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Concerning Benjamin he said:
May the beloved of the LORDrest secure in Him; God shields him all day long, and upon His shoulders he rests.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Concerning Joseph he said:

May his land be blessed by the LORDwith the precious dew from heaven above
and the deep waters that lie beneath,
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: with the bountiful harvest from the sun and the abundant yield of the seasons,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: with the best of the ancient mountains and the bounty of the everlasting hills,
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: with the choice gifts of the land and everything in it, and with the favor of Him who dwelt in the burning bush. May these rest on the head of Joseph and crown the brow of the prince of his brothers.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: His majesty is like a firstborn bull, and his horns are like those of a wild ox. With them he will gore the nations, even to the ends of the earth. Such are the myriads of Ephraim, and such are the thousands of Manasseh.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Concerning Zebulun he said:
Rejoice, Zebulun, in your journeys, and Issachar, in your tents.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: They will call the peoples to a mountain; there they will offer sacrifices of righteousness. For they will feast on the abundance of the seas and the hidden treasures of the sand.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text: Concerning Gad he said:
Blessed is he who enlargesthe domain of Gad! He lies down like a lion and tears off an arm or a head.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
He chose the best land for himself, because a ruler’s portion was reserved for him there. He came with the leaders of the people; he administered the LORD’s justice
and His ordinances for Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Concerning Dan he said:
Dan is a lion’s cub, leaping out of Bashan.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Concerning Naphtali he said:
Naphtali is abounding with favor, full of the blessing of the LORD; he shall take possessionof the sea and the south.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: And concerning Asher he said:

May Asher be the most blessed of sons; may he be the most favored among his brothers
and dip his foot in oil.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: May the bolts of your gate be iron and bronze, and may your strength match your days.
segments.speaker: Moses

segments.text:
There is none like the God of Jeshurun, who rides the heavens to your aid, 
and the clouds in His majesty.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: The eternal God is your dwelling place, and underneath are the everlasting arms. He drives out the enemy before you, giving the command, ‘Destroy him!’
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So Israel dwells securely; the fountain of Jacob lives untroubledin a land of grain and new wine, where even the heavens drip with dew.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: Blessed are you, O Israel! Who is like you, a people saved by the LORD? He is the shield that protects you, the sword in which you boast. Your enemies will cower before you, and you shall trample their high places.
segments.speaker: Moses
